summary: A solid 1972 performance highlighted by a monster The Other One, but the audience‑sourced recording is uneven and scratchy.
review: Fans agree the show captures the energy of the Strand run with an especially powerful The Other One and decent Truckin'. However, the tape suffers from mic‑stand rumble, high‑frequency hiss and occasional drop‑outs, making it only fair for serious listening. Overall the music shines through enough to recommend streaming despite the technical flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance (especially The Other One) while consistently criticizing the recording quality; weighting 70 % show and 30 % audio yields a mid‑high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Scratchy high‑frequency noise, occasional mic‑stand rumble, cut sections (SMBH), but instruments are generally audible; better than some other ’72 tapes yet far from pristine.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical late‑tour Strand run with strong energy; the second set builds well and the performance feels representative of the ’72 European tour.
